quote: we identified and characterized a novel secreted protein, the extracellular serine protease EspP, which is encoded by the large plasmid of enterohaemorrhagic Escherichia coli (EHEC) O157:H7. ... It can be grouped into the autotransporter protein family.
note: SPATE serine-protease autotransporter; plasmid pO157-encoded (RefSeq NC_002128.1, locus pO157p78 in Sakai).

quote: Neisseria meningitidis secretes a protease that specifically cleaves the hinge region of immunoglobulin A1 (IgA1)
note: IgA1 protease, the founding classical autotransporter (PF03797; Pohlner 1987 Nature 10.1038/325458a0 defined the AT mechanism in N. gonorrhoeae). Locus NMB0700 in N. meningitidis MC58; primary_ref is the MC58-lineage characterization.

quote: Antigen 43 is a surface-displayed autotransporter protein that mediates bacterial self-association and pathogenicity.
note: Antigen 43 (Ag43) classical self-associating autotransporter; locus b2000/flu in MG1655.

quote: Hap is an H. influenzae serine protease autotransporter protein that mediates adherence, invasion and microcolony formation in assays with human epithelial cells
note: Hap adhesion-and-penetration serine-protease autotransporter; locus HI_0248 in Rd KW20.

quote: We have identified and characterized a secreted protein, designated Pic ... The deduced amino acid sequence of Pic shows high homology to autotransporter proteins, particularly a subgroup termed the SPATEs (serine protease autotransporters of the Enterobacteriaceae).
note: Pic SPATE serine-protease/mucinase autotransporter; locus EC042_4593 in EAEC 042 (also c0350 in UPEC CFT073).

quote: The secreted autotransporter toxin (Sat) of uropathogenic Escherichia coli exhibits cytopathic activity ... Sat, a vacuolating cytotoxin expressed by uropathogenic E. coli CFT073
note: Sat SPATE secreted autotransporter toxin; locus c3619 in CFT073.

quote: The type V secretion pathway encompasses the autotransporter proteins, the two-partner secretion system, and the recently described type Vc or AT-2 family of proteins.
note: Tsh/Hbp SPATE serine-protease autotransporter (= Hbp hemoglobin protease); plasmid pAPEC-O1-ColBM (RefSeq NC_009838.1), locus APECO1_O1CoBM73.

quote: Several of these proteins play essential roles in the pathogenesis of bacterial infections and have been characterized in detail, demonstrating a diverse array of function including the ability to condense host cell actin
note: IcsA/VirG classical autotransporter (actin-based motility); virulence plasmid pCP301 (RefSeq NC_004851.1), locus CP0182.

quote: The type V secretion pathway encompasses the autotransporter proteins
note: Pertactin (P.69) classical autotransporter; locus BP1054 in Tohama I.

organism: Neisseria meningitidis serogroup B str. MC58
quote: NadA has a predicted molecular structure strikingly similar to a novel class of adhesins (YadA and UspA2), forms high molecular weight oligomers, and binds to epithelial cells in vitro
note: NadA trimeric autotransporter adhesin (YadA-like, PF03895); locus NMB1994 in MC58.

quote: The non-fimbrial adhesins, YadA of enteropathogenic Yersinia species, and UspA1 and UspA2 of Moraxella catarrhalis ... reveal a novel class of adhesins ... a tripartite organization: an N-terminal oval head domain ... a putative coiled-coil rod and ... a C-terminal membrane anchor domain.
note: YadA trimeric autotransporter adhesin (prototype TAA); virulence plasmid pYVe8081 (RefSeq NC_008791.1), locus YEP0066.

quote: the type Vc or AT-2 family of proteins
note: SadA trimeric autotransporter adhesin (Salmonella adhesin A); locus STM3691 in LT2.

quote: a nonpilus protein called Hsf ... shares significant sequence similarity with the nontypeable H. influenzae Hia autotransporter ... our understanding of the architecture of Hsf and the family of trimeric autotransporters
note: Hia trimeric autotransporter adhesin; locus_tag NTHI1209_01464 from the strain 1209 WGS annotation (assembly GCF; no single NC_ chromosome accession, refseq_genome left blank). Reference also covers Hsf (same TAA family in H. influenzae type b).

quote: We discovered a novel secreted protein by Pseudomonas aeruginosa, PlpD ... The association between a TpsB component and a passenger protein is a unique hybrid organization that we propose to classify as Type Vd.
note: PlpD patatin-like hybrid autotransporter, T5dSS type example; locus PA3339 in PAO1.

quote: we conclude that proteins of the intimin and invasin family constitute a novel group of autotransported proteins, and propose that this class of autotransporters be termed Type Ve secretion.
note: Intimin (EaeA), inverse autotransporter T5eSS; locus ECs4559 (Sakai) / Z5110 (EDL933).

quote: Invasin and intimin are major virulence factors of enteropathogenic Yersiniae and Escherichia coli ... type Ve-secreted proteins have an inverted topology with the passenger domain C-terminal to the translocation unit
note: UNPLACEABLE: invasin is the inverse-autotransporter prototype but the exact YE#### chromosomal locus_tag in NC_008800 could not be confirmed from NCBI Gene; locus left blank rather than guessed.

quote: Like the prototypic Yersinia adhesin A, BadA belongs to the class of trimeric autotransporter adhesins and is constructed modularly consisting of a head, a long and repetitive neck-stalk module and a membrane anchor.
note: UNPLACEABLE: BadA characterized in B. henselae Marseille (GenBank DQ665674, no locus_tag); the Houston-1 RefSeq (NC_005956.1) BadA locus could not be cleanly confirmed (NCBI Gene record discontinued). Locus left blank.

quote: UspA1 and UspA2 of Moraxella catarrhalis, are established pathogenicity factors ... reveal a novel class of adhesins
note: UNPLACEABLE: UspA1 ubiquitous surface protein, trimeric autotransporter; UniProt entries carry no ordered locus and Moraxella strains vary, so no RefSeq locus_tag could be confirmed without guessing. Locus left blank.
